$comment: DEVLP-159: registry of Botpress/@bpinternal upstream versions our forks were cut from. There is no single place these lived before this file — reconstructed from README.md, docs/adr/0005, git history (fork commits e3694c9/f4a8963/38d2c83/6495425), and in-source 'ported from' comments (grep the paths under "source" to re-derive). Do NOT invent a version for an entry you cannot trace back to one of those; use pinned:null and explain in note instead. scripts/upstream-watch.mjs reads this file weekly and diffs `pinned` against the live npm `latest` for each non-null `upstream`.

forks.source: README.md (runtime libraries table); docs/adr/0005-opapi-as-source-of-truth.md (byte-oracle: index.d.ts must byte-equal this exact version)
README.md; commit f4a8963 ('fork @botpress/sdk@6.13.0')
README.md; CHANGELOG.md; commit e3694c9 ('fork @bpinternal/zui@2.3.0 from source')
README.md (vendored dist, not the byte oracle)
forks.note: vendored dist, not source-forked — a bump still needs a manual re-vendor, check-drift.sh does not cover it.

forks.source: README.md; CHANGELOG.md; commit e3694c9
README.md; commit e3694c9 (vendored dist, VRL/wasm executor)
README.md; commit e3694c9 (vendored dist)
commit e3694c9 (reimplement the single used symbol) and commit 6495425 ('+ FileId + limitConfigs from @bpinternal/const 0.24.0')
forks.note: NOT a byte-fork — reimplements only the handful of symbols brt/runtime actually use (prefixToObjectMap, FileId, limitConfigs). A drift alert here means 'go check whether upstream added/changed one of those symbols', not 'resync the whole package'.

forks.source: docs/adr/0005-opapi-as-source-of-truth.md; live build-time dependency in packages/botruntime-api/package.json (self-verifying — cross-check that file directly, this pin should never drift from it without a matching ADR-0005 update)
commit 38d2c83 ('136 src files reconstructed from npm bundle maps' of @botpress/adk@2.0.2)
commit 38d2c83 ('npm source, 86 tests pass, leaf')
commit 38d2c83 names the upstream package but only records our own fork's initial version (0.1.0), not the upstream version it was cut from
forks.note: upstream @botpress/analytics returns 404 on the public npm registry (confirmed via `npm view` and the raw registry API) — it looks unpublished/removed, and no commit or doc records which upstream version this minimal fork (AdkError/isAdkError only) was read from. Do not invent one; if the package resurfaces under a different name/scope, re-derive the pin from that source instead.

forks.source: commit 6495425 ('npm source, WASM-inlined; leaf')
commit 6495425 ('clone source; 42 tests pass')
commit 6495425 ('clone source; repoint cognitive/thicktoken/zui')
commit 6495425 ('clone source; repoint client/cognitive/thicktoken/zui')
forks.note: PRECEDENT PACKAGE for DEVLP-159 (the pnpm/bun patch on source-map-js@1.2.1 was lost on this exact fork and the prod bot went silent on every turn until 52afff9 re-applied it). Package name on npm is unscoped 'llmz', not '@botpress/llmz'. Commit 52afff9 manually re-applied a patch equivalent to what upstream shipped in llmz@0.0.85, but that was a point patch, not a full source resync — bumping this pin means diffing the ENTIRE range from 0.0.84 to the target release, re-checking packages/botruntime-llmz/patches/source-map-js@1.2.1.patch still applies, not just this one symptom.

forks.source: commit 6495425 ('reconstructed from npm bundle source maps')
commit 6495425 ('115 src files reconstructed from the bundle maps')
README.md and CHANGELOG.md both say 'full fork of @botpress/cli' but neither, nor any commit message (0eca3d4, a69d364, f4a8963), records the upstream version number
forks.note: Sibling packages forked in the same window (client@1.46.0, sdk@6.13.0, chat@0.5.5, zui@2.3.0, all committed 2026-06-30) suggest the CLI fork point is from that same @botpress/cli release train, but that is an inference, not a recorded fact — do not treat it as a pin. Needs a one-time manual reconciliation (e.g. diff @botpress/cli's published versions around 2026-06-30) before this entry can carry a real version.

forks.source: integrations/telegram/integration.definition.ts:5 ('Originally ported from @botpresshub/telegram v1.0.9'); initial package.json description at commit 0eca3d4
forks.note: upstream @botpresshub/telegram also returns 404 on the public npm registry (confirmed) — looks unpublished/moved/renamed. The 1.0.9 pin is real (it is the recorded fork point) but there is currently nothing live to diff it against; the watch script reports this as 'upstream unavailable' rather than silently treating it as 'no drift'.
